description: Extract shared InterventionPlannerStep from lease-interventions-step.tsx. Make it reusable for supplier contract reminders with configurable assignable roles, scheduling options, and custom interventions.

userStories.title: Shared types + assignable roles constants
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared types (AssignableRole, InterventionPlannerSection, InterventionPlannerStepProps) and role constants, so both lease and supplier wizards can configure the planner.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/types/intervention-planner.types.ts created with all interfaces
lib/constants/assignable-roles.ts created with LEASE/SUPPLIER role configs + ROLE_TO_CONTACT_TYPE + CONTACT_TYPE_TO_ROLE mappings
Lint passes

userStories.title: Supplier scheduling options + intervention factory
userStories.description: As a developer, I want supplier-specific scheduling options (relative to end date / préavis) and a factory to create ScheduledInterventionData from supplier contracts.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/constants/supplier-interventions.ts created
getSupplierSchedulingOptions returns options relative to endDate
Notice period option added when noticePeriodValue > 0
Default option is notice_date when available, else end_date
createSupplierReminderIntervention produces valid ScheduledInterventionData
Lint passes

userStories.title: InterventionScheduleRow: configurable assignableRoles prop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want InterventionScheduleRow to accept an optional assignableRoles prop so the assign popover shows only the relevant roles for each context.
acceptanceCriteria: assignableRoles prop added (optional, backward-compatible default)
Popover uses assignableRoles instead of hardcoded ASSIGN_TYPE_OPTIONS
Existing lease behavior unchanged (no prop = all 3 roles)
Lint passes

userStories.title: InterventionPlannerStep shared wrapper component
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a shared InterventionPlannerStep that handles header, ScrollArea, ContactSelector, assignment state, and section rendering, so both lease and supplier wizards reuse the same orchestration logic.
acceptanceCriteria: components/contract/intervention-planner-step.tsx created (~250 lines)
Manages contactSelectorRef + activeAssignment state
handleToggle, handleDateChange, handleSchedulingOptionChange work
handleContactSelected/Removed work for both intervention rows and external keys (rent reminders)
Section rendering loop with Separators, section headers with icons, allowCustomAdd button
renderCustom sections rendered correctly
Lint passes

userStories.title: Refactor LeaseInterventionsStep to thin wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want LeaseInterventionsStep to delegate to InterventionPlannerStep, keeping only lease-specific logic (rent reminders, section building, custom CRUD, tenant pre-selection).
acceptanceCriteria: LeaseInterventionsStep reduced from ~540 to ~180 lines
Builds 4 sections (custom, rent, standard, documents) and passes to InterventionPlannerStep
Rent reminder block preserved via renderCustom
All existing lease wizard functionality works identically
Custom intervention add/delete/edit works
ContactSelector assignment works for all sections including rent reminders
Lint passes

userStories.title: Supplier reminders step using InterventionPlannerStep
userStories.description: As a gestionnaire, I want the supplier contract reminder step to use InterventionPlannerStep with full features: scheduling options, contact assignment, and custom reminders.
acceptanceCriteria: State changed from Record<string, ReminderConfig> to ScheduledInterventionData[]
useEffect syncs interventions from supplierContracts (preserving user edits)
Case 2 renders InterventionPlannerStep with supplier sections
Custom reminders section with + Ajouter button
Contract reminders with scheduling dropdown (end date / préavis options)
Assign button shows only Gestionnaires + Prestataires
Submission converts ScheduledInterventionData[] back to Record format for server action
Lint passes
